servinfo.title: Maintenance Schedule under Normal Driving Conditions

item.ptxt: The intervals should be determined based on odometer readings or months, whichever comes first.
The following table shows the service schedule up to 90,000 km (54,000 miles). Beyond 90,000 km (54,000 miles), perform the same maintenance services at the same intervals shown in the table.
This table shows the typical maintenance schedule.
The actual maintenance schedule, however, varies depending on vehicle specifications, so the typical maintenance schedule may not be applicable to some models.

Therefore, for the maintenance schedule that is specifically applicable to the vehicle being serviced, follow the relevant instructions in the “Periodic Maintenance Schedule” under “INSPECTION AND MAINTENANCE” section of the Owner’s Manual.
Class 1: Petrol engine with oxygen sensor
Class 2: Petrol engine without oxygen sensor

entry.ptxt:
Engine oil and engine oil filter (R:
)
SUZUKI long life coolant: Standard (Coolant color: Green)
SUZUKI long life coolant: Super (Coolant color: Blue)*1
First time only: Replace at 150,000 km (90,000 miles) or 96 months
Second time and after: Replace every 75,000 km (45,000 miles) or 48 months
Replace every 105,000 km (63,000 miles) or 84 months.
When leaded fuel is used, refer to Maintenance Recommended under Severe Driving Conditions schedule.
Refer to Maintenance Recommended under Severe Driving Conditions schedule.

Brake discs and pads (thickness, wear, damage) (I:
)

Brake hoses and pipes (leakage, damage, clamp) (I:
)

Brake lever and cable (damage, stroke, operation) (I:
)
Replace every 150,000 km (90,000 miles) or 120 months.
Other than “SUZUKI GEAR OIL 75W-80” (I: 1st 15,000 km only)

item.ptxt: “I”: Inspect, clean, adjust, lubricate or replace if necessary
*1: If SUZUKI super long life coolant (coolant color: Blue) is replaced with the other coolant, follow schedule for SUZUKI long life coolant (coolant color: Green).
For specified spark plug, refer to Spark Plug Inspection .
*2: For “Nickel plugs”, replace every 50,000 km if the local law requires.

Periodic replacement of fuel filter is not necessary if it is not instructed in “Periodic Maintenance Schedule” section of the Owner’s manual. The scheduled maintenance varies depending on the vehicle specification.
